rulings.Case Title: CAROL HAYNES et al VS. BASF CORPORATION INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR IN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories, Inspection Demand, And Form Interrogatories; And To Deem Admitted The Truth Of The Matters In Crane Co.S Request For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS DISCOVERY CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, OCTOBER 3, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 at 9:00 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion to compel responses to special interrogatories, inspection demand and form interrogatories and to deem admitted the truth of the matters in defendant¿s requests for admissions is granted. No opposition filed. Plaintiffs have 10 days to serve responses to special interrogatories, inspection demands and form interrogatories. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
